section.name: env-file-patterns
section.summary: .env file format rules, systemd EnvironmentFile integration constraints, and file permission requirements.

rule.statement: Set chmod 600 on every .env file immediately after creating it; world-readable secrets are a breach waiting to happen.
rule.rationale: Default umask creates files with mode 644. A world-readable .env containing API keys or database credentials can be read by any process on the server, including compromised services.

rule.statement: Never use the export prefix in .env files intended for systemd EnvironmentFile; systemd does not parse shell syntax, only bare KEY=VALUE pairs.
rule.rationale: export DATABASE_URL=... is valid shell syntax but systemd EnvironmentFile treats the word "export" as part of the variable name, creating a variable literally named "export DATABASE_URL".

rule.statement: Never expand variables in .env values ($OTHER_VAR); systemd EnvironmentFile does not perform variable substitution.
rule.rationale: REDIS_URL=redis://$HOST:6379 will be passed literally with "$HOST" as a string, not expanded. Write full literal values in .env files for systemd consumption.

example.code:
# .env — valid format for systemd EnvironmentFile # Comments with # are allowed # No spaces around = # No export prefix # Quote values containing spaces or special characters # Literal values only (no $VAR expansion)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://nero:secretpass@localhost:5432/nero_db REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx JWT_SECRET=a-long-random-string-here RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://nero:rabbitpass@localhost:5672/nero LOG_LEVEL=INFO DEBUG=false

# Wrong: export prefix breaks systemd EnvironmentFile export DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost/nero_db
# Wrong: variable expansion silently fails in systemd HOST=localhost DB_PORT=5432 DATABASE_URL=postgresql://$HOST:$DB_PORT/nero_db
# Wrong: spaces around = break the parse DATABASE_URL = postgresql://localhost/nero_db
example.note: All three patterns above work in a shell (source .env) but silently fail or produce wrong values when loaded by systemd EnvironmentFile.

rule.statement: Use the - prefix (EnvironmentFile=-/path) for optional override files; required files without - will fail the service start if missing.
rule.rationale: A missing required EnvironmentFile causes the systemd unit to fail with "No such file or directory" before the process even starts. The - prefix makes the file optional, useful for per-instance overrides.

example.code:
[Service] # Required: service fails to start if this is missing EnvironmentFile=/srv/nero/nero-core/.env
# Optional: loaded if present, ignored if absent EnvironmentFile=-/srv/nero/nero-core/.env.local
ExecStart=/srv/nero/nero-core/.venv/bin/python -m src.main

list.item: ~/workspace/.env — master secrets file, source of truth for all credentials
.env.example — committed to git, shows variable names with placeholder values
.env.tpl — 1Password inject template with op:// references; safe to commit
